summary: Operators trigger a palette run that executes the workspace-root full sync helper with `{}` stdin for the Cursor hook envelope, then should see a rundown of synced plugin bundles plus reload guidance. Eval cases should insist on matching that execution contract and recap.

cases.scenario: palette full sync honours root node helper and stdin
cases.expected_output: The agent runs `node .cursor/hooks/sync-plugins.mjs --full` from the repository root feeding `{}` on stdin without errors and closes with readable plugin identifiers plus reload guidance aligned with stdout.
assertions: after `/sync-plugins`, the manifested terminal or subprocess exits 0 running `node .cursor/hooks/sync-plugins.mjs --full` from the workspace root with `{}` provided on stdin exactly as instructed
closing guidance names each synced plugin artifact that the helper reported so the caller can correlate disk output with this repository
closing guidance mentions that reloading Cursor surfaces the freshly copied bundles

cases.scenario: post-sync user recap emphasises enumerated bundles and reload
cases.prompt: /sync-plugins — local plugin copies should match the workspace after I edited multiple plugins.
cases.expected_output: The reply stays anchored to stdout from the helper, calls out synced plugin bundles explicitly, and reiterates the reload prerequisite before depending on behavioural changes in another editor window.
assertions: the assistant never skips listing the plugin identifiers surfaced by `.cursor/hooks/sync-plugins.mjs --full` when that output is returned
reload instructions appear in prose so the caller knows behavioural updates require reloading Cursor windows after the filesystem sync finishes
the assistant cites success or surfaced errors from that root-level node invocation rather than inventing phantom sync progress
